instruction: Create comprehensive unit tests with pytest for a UserService including mocking, fixtures, and parametrization.
output:
```python
import pytest
from unittest.mock import Mock, patch, MagicMock
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import uuid

from app.services.user_service import UserService
from app.models.user import User, UserStatus
from app.exceptions import UserNotFoundError, DuplicateEmailError


# Fixtures
@pytest.fixture
def mock_user_repository():
    """Create mock user repository."""
    return Mock()

@pytest.fixture
def mock_email_service():
    """Create mock email service."""
    return Mock()

@pytest.fixture
def mock_cache():
    """Create mock cache client."""
    return Mock()

@pytest.fixture
def user_service(mock_user_repository, mock_email_service, mock_cache):
    """Create UserService with mocked dependencies."""
    return UserService(
        user_repo=mock_user_repository,
        email_service=mock_email_service,
        cache=mock_cache
    )

@pytest.fixture
def sample_user():
    """Create sample user for testing."""
    return User(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        email="test@example.com",
        first_name="John",
        last_name="Doe",
        status=UserStatus.ACTIVE,
        created_at=datetime.utcnow()
    )


class TestUserServiceCreate:
    """Test user creation functionality."""
    
    def test_create_user_success(
        self, 
        user_service, 
        mock_user_repository, 
        mock_email_service
    ):
        """Should create user and send welcome email."""
        # Arrange
        email = "newuser@example.com"
        password = "SecurePass123!"
        first_name = "Jane"
        last_name = "Smith"
        
        mock_user_repository.get_by_email.return_value = None
        mock_user_repository.create.return_value = User(
            id=uuid.uuid4(),
            email=email,
            first_name=first_name,
            last_name=last_name,
            status=UserStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION
        )
        
        # Act
        result = user_service.create_user(
            email=email,
            password=password,
            first_name=first_name,
            last_name=last_name
        )
        
        # Assert
        assert result.email == email
        assert result.status == UserStatus.PENDING_VERIFICATION
        mock_user_repository.create.assert_called_once()
        mock_email_service.send_welcome_email.assert_called_once_with(email)
    
    def test_create_user_duplicate_email(
        self,
        user_service,
        mock_user_repository,
        sample_user
    ):
        """Should raise error when email already exists."""
        # Arrange
        mock_user_repository.get_by_email.return_value = sample_user
        
        # Act & Assert
        with pytest.raises(DuplicateEmailError) as exc_info:
            user_service.create_user(
                email=sample_user.email,
                password="password123",
                first_name="Test",
                last_name="User"
            )
        
        assert str(sample_user.email) in str(exc_info.value)
        mock_user_repository.create.assert_not_called()
    
    @pytest.mark.parametrize("invalid_email", [
        "invalid-email",
        "@example.com",
        "user@",
        "user..name@example.com",
        ""
    ])
    def test_create_user_invalid_email(
        self,
        user_service,
        invalid_email
    ):
        """Should validate email format."""
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid email format"):
            user_service.create_user(
                email=invalid_email,
                password="password123",
                first_name="Test",
                last_name="User"
            )


class TestUserServiceAuthentication:
    """Test authentication functionality."""
    
    @patch('app.services.user_service.verify_password')
    def test_authenticate_success(
        self,
        mock_verify,
        user_service,
        mock_user_repository,
        sample_user
    ):
        """Should authenticate with valid credentials."""
        # Arrange
        mock_user_repository.get_by_email.return_value = sample_user
        mock_verify.return_value = True
        
        # Act
        result = user_service.authenticate(
            email=sample_user.email,
            password="correct_password"
        )
        
        # Assert
        assert result == sample_user
        mock_verify.assert_called_once_with("correct_password", sample_user.password_hash)
    
    @patch('app.services.user_service.verify_password')
    def test_authenticate_invalid_password(
        self,
        mock_verify,
        user_service,
        mock_user_repository,
        sample_user
    ):
        """Should fail with invalid password."""
        mock_user_repository.get_by_email.return_value = sample_user
        mock_verify.return_value = False
        
        result = user_service.authenticate(
            email=sample_user.email,
            password="wrong_password"
        )
        
        assert result is None
    
    def test_authenticate_nonexistent_user(
        self,
        user_service,
        mock_user_repository
    ):
        """Should return None for non-existent user."""
        mock_user_repository.get_by_email.return_value = None
        
        result = user_service.authenticate(
            email="nonexistent@example.com",
            password="password123"
        )
        
        assert result is None


class TestUserServiceCaching:
    """Test caching behavior."""
    
    def test_get_user_uses_cache(
        self,
        user_service,
        mock_cache,
        mock_user_repository,
        sample_user
    ):
        """Should return cached user if available."""
        # Arrange
        mock_cache.get.return_value = sample_user
        
        # Act
        result = user_service.get_user(sample_user.id)
        
        # Assert
        assert result == sample_user
        mock_cache.get.assert_called_once_with(f"user:{sample_user.id}")
        mock_user_repository.get_by_id.assert_not_called()
    
    def test_get_user_populates_cache(
        self,
        user_service,
        mock_cache,
        mock_user_repository,
        sample_user
    ):
        """Should cache user after DB fetch."""
        mock_cache.get.return_value = None
        mock_user_repository.get_by_id.return_value = sample_user
        
        result = user_service.get_user(sample_user.id)
        
        assert result == sample_user
        mock_cache.set.assert_called_once_with(
            f"user:{sample_user.id}",
            sample_user,
            ttl=300
        )
    
    def test_update_user_invalidates_cache(
        self,
        user_service,
        mock_cache,
        mock_user_repository,
        sample_user
    ):
        """Should invalidate cache on update."""
        mock_user_repository.update.return_value = sample_user
        
        user_service.update_user(
            user_id=sample_user.id,
            first_name="Updated"
        )
        
        mock_cache.delete.assert_called_once_with(f"user:{sample_user.id}")


class TestUserServiceAsync:
    """Test async operations."""
    
    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_send_bulk_emails(
        self,
        user_service,
        mock_email_service
    ):
        """Should send emails to multiple users."""
        user_ids = [uuid.uuid4() for _ in range(5)]
        mock_email_service.send_email_async = Mock(return_value=asyncio.Future())
        mock_email_service.send_email_async.return_value.set_result(None)
        
        with patch.object(user_service, '_get_user_emails', return_value=["user@example.com"]):
            result = await user_service.send_bulk_emails(
                user_ids=user_ids,
                subject="Test",
                body="Hello"
            )
        
        assert result['sent'] == 5
        assert mock_email_service.send_email_async.call_count == 5


# Integration-style test with test database
@pytest.mark.integration
class TestUserServiceIntegration:
    """Integration tests with real database."""
    
    def test_user_workflow(self, db_session):
        """Test complete user lifecycle."""
        service = UserService(
            user_repo=SQLAlchemyUserRepository(db_session),
            email_service=MockEmailService(),
            cache=NullCache()
        )
        
        # Create
        user = service.create_user(
            email="integration@test.com",
            password="SecurePass123!",
            first_name="Integration",
            last_name="Test"
        )
        assert user.id is not None
        
        # Read
        fetched = service.get_user(user.id)
        assert fetched.email == "integration@test.com"
        
        # Update
        updated = service.update_user(user.id, first_name="Updated")
        assert updated.first_name == "Updated"
        
        # Delete
        service.delete_user(user.id)
        assert service.get_user(user.id) is None
```

instruction: Write integration tests for a REST API using TestContainers for PostgreSQL and Redis.
output:
```python
import pytest
import asyncio
from httpx import AsyncClient
from testcontainers.postgres import PostgresContainer
from testcontainers.redis import RedisContainer

from app.main import create_app
from app.database import init_db, close_db
from app.cache import init_cache, close_cache


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def postgres_container():
    """Start PostgreSQL container."""
    with PostgresContainer("postgres:15-alpine") as postgres:
        yield postgres


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def redis_container():
    """Start Redis container."""
    with RedisContainer("redis:7-alpine") as redis:
        yield redis


@pytest.fixture
async def app(postgres_container, redis_container):
    """Create app with test database and cache."""
    # Override config with container URLs
    test_config = {
        'DATABASE_URL': postgres_container.get_connection_url(),
        'REDIS_URL': redis_container.get_connection_url()
    }
    
    app = create_app(config=test_config)
    
    # Initialize connections
    await init_db(test_config['DATABASE_URL'])
    await init_cache(test_config['REDIS_URL'])
    
    yield app
    
    # Cleanup
    await close_cache()
    await close_db()


@pytest.fixture
async def client(app):
    """Create async HTTP client."""
    async with AsyncClient(app=app, base_url="http://test") as client:
        yield client


@pytest.fixture
async def auth_headers(client):
    """Get authentication headers."""
    # Register and login
    await client.post("/auth/register", json={
        "email": "test@example.com",
        "password": "SecurePass123!",
        "name": "Test User"
    })
    
    response = await client.post("/auth/login", json={
        "email": "test@example.com",
        "password": "SecurePass123!"
    })
    
    token = response.json()['access_token']
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}


class TestAuthEndpoints:
    """Test authentication endpoints."""
    
    async def test_register_success(self, client):
        """Should register new user."""
        response = await client.post("/auth/register", json={
            "email": "newuser@example.com",
            "password": "SecurePass123!",
            "name": "New User"
        })
        
        assert response.status_code == 201
        data = response.json()
        assert data['email'] == "newuser@example.com"
        assert 'id' in data
        assert 'password' not in data
    
    async def test_register_duplicate_email(self, client):
        """Should reject duplicate email."""
        # First registration
        await client.post("/auth/register", json={
            "email": "duplicate@example.com",
            "password": "SecurePass123!",
            "name": "User One"
        })
        
        # Duplicate
        response = await client.post("/auth/register", json={
            "email": "duplicate@example.com",
            "password": "AnotherPass123!",
            "name": "User Two"
        })
        
        assert response.status_code == 409
        assert "already exists" in response.json()['detail'].lower()
    
    async def test_login_success(self, client):
        """Should authenticate valid user."""
        # Register first
        await client.post("/auth/register", json={
            "email": "login@test.com",
            "password": "SecurePass123!",
            "name": "Login Test"
        })
        
        # Login
        response = await client.post("/auth/login", json={
            "email": "login@test.com",
            "password": "SecurePass123!"
        })
        
        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = response.json()
        assert 'access_token' in data
        assert 'refresh_token' in data
        assert data['token_type'] == 'bearer'
    
    async def test_login_invalid_credentials(self, client):
        """Should reject invalid credentials."""
        response = await client.post("/auth/login", json={
            "email": "nonexistent@example.com",
            "password": "WrongPassword!"
        })
        
        assert response.status_code == 401


class TestCRUDEndpoints:
    """Test CRUD operations with auth."""
    
    async def test_create_item(self, client, auth_headers):
        """Should create item with auth."""
        response = await client.post("/items/",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Test Item",
                "description": "A test item",
                "price": 29.99
            }
        )
        
        assert response.status_code == 201
        data = response.json()
        assert data['name'] == "Test Item"
        assert float(data['price']) == 29.99
    
    async def test_get_items(self, client, auth_headers):
        """Should list items."""
        # Create items
        for i in range(3):
            await client.post("/items/",
                headers=auth_headers,
                json={
                    "name": f"Item {i}",
                    "description": "Test",
                    "price": 10.0 * (i + 1)
                }
            )
        
        response = await client.get("/items/", headers=auth_headers)
        
        assert response.status_code == 200
        data = response.json()
        assert len(data['items']) >= 3
        assert 'total' in data
    
    async def test_update_item(self, client, auth_headers):
        """Should update item."""
        # Create
        create_response = await client.post("/items/",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Original",
                "description": "Test",
                "price": 10.0
            }
        )
        item_id = create_response.json()['id']
        
        # Update
        update_response = await client.put(
            f"/items/{item_id}",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Updated",
                "description": "Updated desc",
                "price": 20.0
            }
        )
        
        assert update_response.status_code == 200
        assert update_response.json()['name'] == "Updated"
    
    async def test_delete_item(self, client, auth_headers):
        """Should delete item."""
        # Create
        create_response = await client.post("/items/",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "To Delete",
                "description": "Will be deleted",
                "price": 5.0
            }
        )
        item_id = create_response.json()['id']
        
        # Delete
        delete_response = await client.delete(
            f"/items/{item_id}",
            headers=auth_headers
        )
        
        assert delete_response.status_code == 204
        
        # Verify deleted
        get_response = await client.get(
            f"/items/{item_id}",
            headers=auth_headers
        )
        assert get_response.status_code == 404
    
    async def test_unauthorized_access(self, client):
        """Should reject unauthenticated requests."""
        response = await client.get("/items/")
        assert response.status_code == 401


class TestCacheBehavior:
    """Test Redis caching."""
    
    async def test_cache_hit(self, client, auth_headers, redis_container):
        """Should use cached data."""
        # Create item
        create_resp = await client.post("/items/",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Cached Item",
                "description": "Test",
                "price": 15.0
            }
        )
        item_id = create_resp.json()['id']
        
        # First request (DB hit)
        start = asyncio.get_event_loop().time()
        await client.get(f"/items/{item_id}", headers=auth_headers)
        db_time = asyncio.get_event_loop().time() - start
        
        # Second request (cache hit)
        start = asyncio.get_event_loop().time()
        await client.get(f"/items/{item_id}", headers=auth_headers)
        cache_time = asyncio.get_event_loop().time() - start
        
        # Cache should be faster
        assert cache_time < db_time
    
    async def test_cache_invalidation(self, client, auth_headers):
        """Should invalidate cache on update."""
        # Create and fetch
        create_resp = await client.post("/items/",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Cache Test",
                "description": "Original",
                "price": 10.0
            }
        )
        item_id = create_resp.json()['id']
        
        # Fetch (caches)
        await client.get(f"/items/{item_id}", headers=auth_headers)
        
        # Update (should invalidate)
        await client.put(
            f"/items/{item_id}",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Cache Test",
                "description": "Updated",
                "price": 20.0
            }
        )
        
        # Fetch again (should get updated value, not cached)
        get_resp = await client.get(f"/items/{item_id}", headers=auth_headers)
        assert get_resp.json()['description'] == "Updated"


class TestDatabaseTransactions:
    """Test database transaction behavior."""
    
    async def test_rollback_on_error(self, client, auth_headers):
        """Should rollback on error."""
        # This endpoint intentionally fails after DB write
        response = await client.post("/items/failing-endpoint",
            headers=auth_headers,
            json={
                "name": "Test",
                "trigger_error": True
            }
        )
        
        assert response.status_code == 500
        
        # Verify data was NOT committed (rolled back)
        list_response = await client.get("/items/", headers=auth_headers)
        items = list_response.json()['items']
        assert not any(i['name'] == 'Test' for i in items)
```

instruction: Create end-to-end tests using Playwright for a React web application with authentication flows.
output:
```python
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect


@pytest.fixture
def login_page(page: Page):
    """Navigate to login page."""
    page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login")
    return page


class TestAuthenticationFlows:
    """Test user authentication flows."""
    
    def test_successful_login(self, login_page: Page):
        """User can login with valid credentials."""
        # Arrange
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=email-input]", "user@example.com")
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=password-input]", "SecurePass123!")
        
        # Act
        login_page.click("[data-testid=login-button]")
        
        # Assert
        expect(login_page).to_have_url("http://localhost:3000/dashboard")
        expect(login_page.locator("[data-testid=user-menu]")).to_be_visible()
    
    def test_login_with_invalid_password(self, login_page: Page):
        """User sees error with invalid password."""
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=email-input]", "user@example.com")
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=password-input]", "wrongpassword")
        login_page.click("[data-testid=login-button]")
        
        # Assert error message
        error_message = login_page.locator("[data-testid=error-message]")
        expect(error_message).to_be_visible()
        expect(error_message).to_contain_text("Invalid credentials")
    
    def test_login_form_validation(self, login_page: Page):
        """Form shows validation errors."""
        # Submit empty form
        login_page.click("[data-testid=login-button]")
        
        # Check validation messages
        email_error = login_page.locator("[data-testid=email-error]")
        password_error = login_page.locator("[data-testid=password-error]")
        
        expect(email_error).to_contain_text("Email is required")
        expect(password_error).to_contain_text("Password is required")
    
    def test_forgot_password_flow(self, login_page: Page):
        """User can request password reset."""
        # Click forgot password
        login_page.click("[data-testid=forgot-password-link]")
        expect(login_page).to_have_url("http://localhost:3000/forgot-password")
        
        # Enter email
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=email-input]", "user@example.com")
        login_page.click("[data-testid=reset-button]")
        
        # Success message
        expect(login_page.locator("[data-testid=success-message]")).to_be_visible()
        expect(login_page.locator("[data-testid=success-message]")).to_contain_text(
            "Check your email"
        )
    
    def test_logout(self, login_page: Page):
        """User can logout."""
        # Login first
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=email-input]", "user@example.com")
        login_page.fill("[data-testid=password-input]", "SecurePass123!")
        login_page.click("[data-testid=login-button]")
        
        # Wait for dashboard
        expect(login_page).to_have_url("http://localhost:3000/dashboard")
        
        # Logout
        login_page.click("[data-testid=user-menu]")
        login_page.click("[data-testid=logout-button]")
        
        # Assert logged out
        expect(login_page).to_have_url("http://localhost:3000/login")
        expect(login_page.locator("[data-testid=login-form]")).to_be_visible()


class TestDashboardFlows:
    """Test dashboard functionality."""
    
    @pytest.fixture
    def dashboard_page(self, page: Page):
        """Login and return dashboard page."""
        page.goto("http://localhost:3000/login")
        page.fill("[data-testid=email-input]", "user@example.com")
        page.fill("[data-testid=password-input]", "SecurePass123!")
        page.click("[data-testid=login-button]")
        expect(page).to_have_url("http://localhost:3000/dashboard")
        return page
    
    def test_dashboard_loads_data(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """Dashboard loads and displays data."""
        # Wait for data to load
        dashboard_page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid=dashboard-stats]")
        
        # Check stats are displayed
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=stat-total-users]")).to_contain_text("Users")
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=stat-revenue]")).to_contain_text("Revenue")
    
    def test_create_new_item(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """User can create new item."""
        # Open create modal
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=create-button]")
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=create-modal]")).to_be_visible()
        
        # Fill form
        dashboard_page.fill("[data-testid=item-name]", "Test Item")
        dashboard_page.fill("[data-testid=item-description]", "Test Description")
        dashboard_page.fill("[data-testid=item-price]", "99.99")
        
        # Submit
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=submit-button]")
        
        # Modal closes and item appears in list
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=create-modal]")).not_to_be_visible()
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("text=Test Item")).to_be_visible()
    
    def test_search_functionality(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """User can search items."""
        # Type in search box
        dashboard_page.fill("[data-testid=search-input]", "specific-item")
        dashboard_page.press("[data-testid=search-input]", "Enter")
        
        # Results update
        dashboard_page.wait_for_selector("[data-testid=search-results]")
        results = dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=result-item]")
        
        # All results contain search term
        count = results.count()
        for i in range(count):
            expect(results.nth(i)).to_contain_text("specific-item")
    
    def test_sort_items(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """User can sort item list."""
        # Click sort dropdown
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=sort-dropdown]")
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=sort-price-desc]")
        
        # Verify sorting (first item has highest price)
        prices = dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=item-price]").all_inner_texts()
        numeric_prices = [float(p.replace('$', '')) for p in prices]
        assert numeric_prices == sorted(numeric_prices, reverse=True)
    
    def test_pagination(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """Pagination works correctly."""
        # Go to next page
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=next-page]")
        
        # URL updates
        expect(dashboard_page).to_have_url(re.compile(r"page=2"))
        
        # Different items shown
        page1_first_item = dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=item-row]").first.inner_text()
        assert "item-11" in page1_first_item or "item-21" in page1_first_item
    
    def test_delete_confirmation(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """Delete shows confirmation dialog."""
        # Click delete on first item
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=delete-button]:first-of-type")
        
        # Confirmation dialog appears
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=confirm-dialog]")).to_be_visible()
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=confirm-dialog]")).to_contain_text(
            "Are you sure"
        )
        
        # Cancel
        dashboard_page.click("[data-testid=cancel-button]")
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=confirm-dialog]")).not_to_be_visible()


class TestResponsiveDesign:
    """Test responsive behavior."""
    
    def test_mobile_navigation(self, page: Page):
        """Mobile menu works correctly."""
        # Set mobile viewport
        page.set_viewport_size({"width": 375, "height": 667})
        page.goto("http://localhost:3000/dashboard")
        
        # Login
        page.fill("[data-testid=email-input]", "user@example.com")
        page.fill("[data-testid=password-input]", "SecurePass123!")
        page.click("[data-testid=login-button]")
        
        # Hamburger menu visible
        expect(page.locator("[data-testid=mobile-menu-button]")).to_be_visible()
        
        # Open menu
        page.click("[data-testid=mobile-menu-button]")
        expect(page.locator("[data-testid=mobile-nav]")).to_be_visible()
        
        # Click link closes menu
        page.click("[data-testid=nav-settings]")
        expect(page.locator("[data-testid=mobile-nav]")).not_to_be_visible()


class TestAccessibility:
    """Accessibility tests."""
    
    def test_login_form_accessibility(self, login_page: Page):
        """Login form is accessible."""
        # Check ARIA labels
        email_input = login_page.locator("[data-testid=email-input]")
        expect(email_input).to_have_attribute("aria-label", "Email address")
        
        # Tab navigation works
        login_page.press("[data-testid=email-input]", "Tab")
        focused = login_page.locator(":focus")
        expect(focused).to_have_attribute("data-testid", "password-input")
    
    def test_keyboard_navigation(self, dashboard_page: Page):
        """Full keyboard navigation support."""
        # Tab through all interactive elements
        dashboard_page.press("body", "Tab")
        dashboard_page.press("body", "Tab")
        dashboard_page.press("body", "Tab")
        
        # Should be on create button
        focused = dashboard_page.locator(":focus")
        expect(focused).to_have_attribute("data-testid", "create-button")
        
        # Press enter activates
        dashboard_page.press("body", "Enter")
        expect(dashboard_page.locator("[data-testid=create-modal]")).to_be_visible()
```
